_comment: 3-scenario matrix (S0, S1, S3) focused on clean, non-oversubscribed deltas. Measurement window is 120 s to tighten stddev. S0 and S1 isolate queen-worker scaling on a fixed 2c/4g PG (1 vs 2 workers, same load). S3 isolates a bigger PG (4c/8g) with 2 queen workers against a doubled client load. All three scenarios stay under host_cpus on a 10-CPU laptop: S0=4, S1=5, S3=7 heavy threads.

defaults._note_max_partitions: Floor. Actual per-scenario value is max(500, 2 x producer.connections) to keep prod:parts <= 1:2 (no write contention). Consumer uses wildcard long-poll so partition count is purely a producer concern.
defaults._note_pg_max_connections: Needs headroom: 200 (sidecar, split across workers) + ~20 (AsyncDbPool, shared) + a few for psql checks. 300 is safe.

scenarios.name: pg2c4g_q1w_prod2w100c
scenarios._note: S0 — single queen worker baseline. The simplest configuration; no intra-worker concurrency. Useful lower bound.

scenarios.name: pg2c4g_q2w_prod2w100c
scenarios._note: S1 — same load as S0, 2 queen workers. Clean delta S0->S1 isolates the effect of adding one queen worker on a fixed PG.

scenarios.name: pg4c8g_q2w_prod4w200c
scenarios._note: S3 — bigger PG (4c/8g), same 2 queen workers, doubled client load. Shows how queen scales when PG has CPU headroom.
